channel.title: Kubai Kevin
channel.description: Practical backend engineering, AI tooling, and developer career advice by Kubai Kevin — a software developer with 10+ years building production systems in fintech and AI.

item.title: Fix LLM vulns: OWASP Top 10 for AI apps
item.description: Cut LLM security incidents 98% with OWASP Top 10 guardrails for 2026 — prompt injection, output sanitization, vector poisoning, and supply chain fixes.

item.title: AI rollouts: feature flags in 2026
item.description: Cut AI rollout latency 84% with feature flags in 2026 — Redis 7.2 cache, PostgreSQL audit trails, and the silent failure you’ll avoid by building observability from day one.

item.title: Build IDPs that don’t crumble in 2026
item.description: Cut platform toil 40% with Backstage 1.26, Argo CD 2.9, and Crossplane 1.15 — real code, latency benchmarks, and the RBAC explosion nobody warns you about.

item.title: Scan 10k repos daily without false positives
item.description: Cut security alerts 98% with reachability analysis — CodeQL 2.17, Semgrep 1.62, and production traffic correlation reduced false positives from 147 to 2.3 per PR

item.title: Stop async Python from burning your budget
item.description: The logs showed no obvious bottlenecks: CPU was 28%, RAM 42%, and the database had spare capacity.

item.title: Engineering interviews: what AI wants now
item.description: Cut engineering hiring failures 70% by testing incident response instead of whiteboard puzzles — concrete interview rubric with Prometheus, Jaeger, and real AWS cost data from Nairobi fintech 2026.

item.title: AI keeps legacy code alive — here’s how
item.description: The migration target was a containerized Go microservice on EKS, but the blocker wasn’t the tech—it was the 300,000 lines of undocumented business logic…

item.title: Why your AI portfolio bombs in Nigeria
item.description: Cut hiring noise 73% with constraint-first filtering — latency under 1s on 3G, recovery under 3s offline, and data cost lines in READMEs. Proven pipeline in 2026.

item.title: LLM evals: the two metrics that bite you
item.description: We measured 87% accuracy on the test set and felt good—until we looked at production logs.

item.title: AI coded my SaaS in 42 days
item.description: Built SaaS in 6 weeks using AI for 80% of code — latency benchmarks, cost breakdown, and the exact prompts that worked in 2026

item.title: Tame audit logs for compliance
item.description: Cut audit log query time 80% — Postgres vs Kafka in 2026 with latency benchmarks, cost breakdowns, and the schema mistake that cost 15 minutes of downtime.

item.title: USSD fintech stack in 2026: build it right
item.description: That’s roughly 20% of the continent’s mobile connections.

item.title: AI codebases: lock it down with strict mode
item.description: Cut production bugs 60% by enforcing strict linting on AI code — ESLint 9.8.0 and ruff 0.5.0 rules that actually work in 2026.

item.title: 5 feature flag tools that became A/B platforms
item.description: Cut API response time 65% with a self-hosted feature flag system — latency, cost, and offline sync numbers for Flagsmith, LaunchDarkly, and PostHog in 2026.

item.title: Alerts 2026: AI turns false alarms to silence
item.description: Cut on-call wake-ups 70% with AI alert triage — Prometheus 2.47, Node 20, and a 128-line service that silences staging noise in 2026

item.title: Survive network chaos: 9 eventuality patterns
item.description: Cut 4.2 % order failures with 9 eventual-consistency patterns — which one saved $28k Black-Friday weekend? Real code, real AWS bills, real mistakes.

item.title: Postgres 17 replaced Redis, Kafka, and Prometheus
item.description: Postgres 17 beats Redis, Kafka, and Prometheus in cost and simplicity — cache, metrics, and events consolidated with 30% lower bills and 60% fewer alerts.

item.title: Run AI SaaS solo in 2026
item.description: Build a solo AI SaaS 5x faster in 2026 — $328 vs $495 monthly cost breakdown, 14k vs 1.2k lines of code, and the SOC 2 compliance trick most miss.

item.title: AI agent postmortems: the three surprises
item.description: Catch AI agent failures 60 % faster with shadowing vs logging — real QPS, cost, and alert-latency numbers from Brazilian, Colombian, and Mexican production systems in 2026.

item.title: AI agents forget overnight — how to keep memory alive
item.description: The agent’s response time stayed under 200ms, so we blamed the retrieval layer.
